openingMessage: We win in this business by being invited back the next day. That doesn't just come from doing the job right — it comes from how we carry ourselves as professionals and as people.
leadershipThought: We don't operate exposed. If there's an issue, call it in. Don't argue. Don't escalate. Execute.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-28):
• Jun 25 night: Marylou & BJ (601/1), Nathan & Dermont (608/8), Jordan & Joseph (606/6), Ralph & James S (605/5), Jacob & Jeremy (604/4), Luis & Gary B (603/3), Robert W & Alfredo (607/7)
• Jun 26 morning: Andrew & Gary (602/2), Dawson & James S (603/3), Ralph & Cindy (605/5), Stefanie & Edward (600/10), Larry & Justin F (604/4), Marylou & Joseph (601/1)
• All non-rig/flowback/workover crews: Andrews shop, 5:30 AM safety meeting
• DL Hutt — Ray Solis on standby; couldn't kill the well, waiting on 2 mud trucks to pump down
• SM Energy #152 — Michael Hutt: back online, pulling tube at 1500 hr
• Meek Lindsey 6F 6H — Cindy Smith & Ralph Forbes arrived ~11 AM
• Rocker B 1 — Nathan Contreras arrived 11:52 AM
• Merchant — Jacob Hernandez: safety meeting done, job started 8:09 AM
• Kendrick #5 — Louie Zarate: job completed, heading back to shop
• Texas Ten — Gage Gonzales: finished for day (Jun 25 evening)
• Ovintiv HSC 320 — Gage Gonzales: done for day, resuming Monday 7:00 AM
• H&P 379 — Freddy V: casing pipe run complete, commencing cement ops; releasing from rig supervision after cement
• Gipple 124H — Dennis Easley: AOL, handoff complete (Jun 26 4:23 AM)
• Shaw 13-8 #183 — Caleb Smith onsite 5:43 AM (Jun 26); David Hammond arrived 5:51 AM
• tx 127 unit 4 — James Stephens: done, headed to next job 7:54 AM
• John Braun — Ralph Forbes: headed there for next job after Meek Lindsey

Carry-Forward:
• **DL Hutt**: still on standby pending 2 mud trucks — no resolution in recording
• **Casing valve leak**: well being bled down — confirm repair and return-to-ops status
• **Ovintiv HSC 320**: resuming Monday 7:00 AM (Gage Gonzales)
• **H&P 379**: cement ops pending, then rig supervision release (Freddy V)
• **Plug and packer job** (unnamed worker): "We'll return Monday"
lessonsLearned:
• Casing valve leak (Stefanie Kirschke, 8:29 AM): Pump trucks arrived to hook up to well. During hookup, operator identified a leak at the casing valve. Operations paused. Crew bleeding down the well with vacuum truck and pump truck before proceeding. Resolution not confirmed in recording.
• Trailer anomaly alert (Ray Solis): "If you notice your trailer running weird or bouncing a lot let us know right away so we can take care of it. This would've been a bad situation if we didn't catch it." — unspecified trailer issue caught before harm; treat as near-miss prompt.
exxonMoments:
Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Operational Discipline)
A blank form submitted late in the day suggests end-of-shift fatigue is eroding the debrief habit — completing the debrief on-site before leaving is more reliable than submitting it after.

Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Operational Discipline)
Blank forms submitted late in the afternoon/evening suggest end-of-day fatigue is a factor in debrief quality — building the debrief into the job closeout on-site is more reliable than completing it later.

Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Two Technicians, Same Location) (Operational Discipline)
Two blank submissions from the same job at the same time is a crew-level debrief gap — the post-job review should be a shared step led by the senior person on site, not an individual task each person submits alone.

Stop Work Authority Exercised — Unsafe Man Basket Operations (Line of Fire)
Man basket work is one of the highest-risk lift operations on an oil and gas site — Stop Work Authority before elevation is always the right call when anything about the setup looks wrong.

Incomplete OWL Submission — Blank Form (Operational Discipline)
Blank forms from the same area across multiple technicians on the same day is a pattern worth addressing at the crew level — this is the fourth blank submission from this region on June 25.

Casing valve leak
fieldNews.detail: (Stefanie Kirschke, 8:29 AM): Pump trucks arrived to hook up to well. During hookup, operator identified a leak at the casing valve. Operations paused. Crew bleeding down the well with vacuum truck and pump truck before proceeding. Resolution not confirmed in recording.

fieldNews.headline: Trailer anomaly alert
fieldNews.detail: (Ray Solis): "If you notice your trailer running weird or bouncing a lot let us know right away so we can take care of it. This would've been a bad situation if we didn't catch it." — unspecified trailer issue caught before harm; treat as near-miss prompt.

todaysChallenge: Find one person today and communicate something early — before they need it.
todaysChallengeSubtext: Call leadership. Notify dispatch. Post in Breakroom. No surprises. That is how trust is built.
